senses.gloss: verbal noun of فَقَرَ (faqara, “to dig; to pierce”) (form I)

examples.text:
10th century, Al-Mutanabbi
وَمَنْ يُنْفِقِ السَّاعَاتِ فِي جَمْعِ مِالِهِ / مَخَافَةَ فَقْرٍ فَٱلَّذِي فَعَلَ الْفَقْرُ
waman yunfiqi s-sāʕāti fī jamʕi mi-l-ihi / maḵāfata faqrin fallaḏī faʕala al-faqru
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

senses.gloss: to break one's neck or spine
to have pain in the vertebrae from injury or illness
verbal noun of فَقَرَ (faqara, “to break one's neck or spine”) (form I)
verbal noun of فَقِرَ (faqira, “to have pain in the vertebrae”) (form I)
plural of فِقْرَة (fiqra), فَقْرَة (faqra), and فُقْرَة (fuqra, “vertebra”)
